entry.title: Re: Is Java in ASE totally not useable because of a bug in the installjava utility?
entry.content:
Hi Ben,
I know it has worked in the past, but it has been many years since I've worked with Java in ASE.
To just do some experimentation, it might work to download an older version of ASE, perhaps even 15.7, and try using that for playing with the feature.
Unfortunately, I don't know when the bug was introduced.
-bret

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 SQL Dashboard User name and password not working
entry.content:
Hi

Please check if issue is same as mentioned in note    3471145 

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK: How to take user roles?
entry.content:
Hi @emorales 

I'm sharing one way of doing it if the backend role plays a significant part in your application’s logic.

  1. Define the Backend Entity (UserInfoSet):
    • Create a backend entity, "UserInfoSet" that will store user role information. This entity will include at least the user identifier and the role name.
  2. Structure of UserInfoSet:
    • Example fields might include:
      • userId (unique identifier for the user)
      • roleName (the role assigned to the user)

  3. Fetching Role Information:

    • Implement a method that reads the role information from "UserInfoSet" when needed, such as during user initialization or session start i.e. in the OnSuccess of your InitializeOnline.action.

  4. Using Role Information:

    • Utilize the fetched role information to manage access or permissions within your application.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Business AI - Certifications, Free Courses, Sample Projects and Many More!
entry.content:
Some of the common queries I receive on LinkedIn are –

  • How to get certified in SAP Business AI?
  • Are there any FREE courses by SAP on these topics?
  • Is there any sample project provided by SAP on AI?
  • Are there any sample codes which I can reuse to build by AI applications?

In this blog, I have tried to compile all these resources for your quick reference.

 

Note

If you are looking for a simple, step-by-step guide to learn SAP Business AI, you may refer to Generative AI with SAP Blog Series. If you are new to generative AI, I will  recommend you to spend few minutes reading the blog series Generative AI for Beginners and 21 Generative AI Jargons Simplified.

 

SAP AI Certifications

Currently, there is one FREE course by SAP, which provides you a Digital Badge as a Record of Achievement.

Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio

In this course, you will learn SAP's Business AI strategy, its principles, ethics, privacy standards, and the opportunities it presents for your business.

 

FREE Courses by SAP on AI and SAP Business AI

Learning how to use the SAP AI Core service on SAP Business Technology Platform

In this course, you will learn how to use SAP AI Core to handle the execution and operation of your artificial intelligence assets.

Generative AI at SAP

This course will help you understand the fundamental uses and benefits of AI in a business context. You will be able to understand the types and value of AI and engage more successfully with AI capabilities in SAP products and solutions.

Navigating Large Language Models fundamentals and techniques for your use case

This course will provide you a description of Large Language Models (LLMs) and SAP's Business AI strategy towards LLMs along with techniques and methods for optimal LLM performance for your use case.

 

SAP Discovery Mission – Guided Projects with Step-by-step Instructions and Best Practices

SAP provides these SAP Discovery Mission which includes not just guided projects, but also the reference architecture, step-by-step instructions, resources, best practices, support details and many more.

Here is the list of AI related missions.

Get Started with SAP Business AI

This mission guides you on how to onboard yourself on SAP Business AI.

Get Started with SAP Build Code and Joule using Generative AI (Trial and Enterprise Account)

This mission guides you through the setup of SAP Build Code and shows how to create your first full-stack app in SAP Build Code using Joule and Generative Artificial Intelligence.

Set Up SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub

This mission guides you in setting up SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub.

Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors

This mission guides you to setup SAP Joule with your SAP SuccessFactors instance.

Harnessing Generative AI Capabilities with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine

This mission guides you to leverage the capabilities of SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Generative AI Hub, Azure OpenAI and the Langchain Framework.

Get Started with Document Information Extraction and Generative AI

In this mission, you will learn how generative AI can help you automate use cases for business document processing with LLMs (Large Language Models)

Digitizing Criticality Assessments using SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

This mission is based on a real customer use case about criticality assessments which is currently in productization.

Develop a CAP-based (multitenant) application using GenAI and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)

This mission will guide you on how to build a multitenant application using CAP, leveraging Generative AI Hub and HANA vector engine to implement RAG.

 

Reference Architectures

Retrieval Augmented Generation and Generative AI on SAP BTP

In this reference architecture pattern (multitenant, single tenant, how to seamlessly combine various Large Language Models (LLMs) using the Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core.

Federated Machine Learning

This reference architecture relates to the use case pattern "Federated Machine Learning" of the use case category "Predictive Analytics" as defined by the SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology.

 

Sample Codes on SAP Business AI

Social Media Citizen Reporting using Generative AI Hub

Embedding business context with the SAP HANA Cloud, Vector Engine

Bringing Open-sourced LLMs into SAP AI Core

Generative AI-based code development with Joule in SAP Build Code

 

Additional resources

SAP AI Community

SAP TechEd Group

SAP Learning Journeys

Liked it? Don't forget to give a kudos!

Hope this blog will be helpful for you. If I have missed anything important, please let me know in comment 😊

entry.title: Re: QM Control Key with no Supplier Release Requiring QIR

this doesn't look like a standard message. Can you provide error message number. Select the message row and display additional data.

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now - Software Downloads
entry.content:
You need these 2 only.
SAP Enable Now Author contains the SAP Enable Now Producer msi.

SAP Enable Now Consumpt has the SAP Enable Now Manager, Instant Producer, and the SAP Companion for Desktop.

entry.title: Re: Requisition Import with Attachments Using Web Services
entry.content:
Hi,

I have similar setup but getting error while trying to import requisition with attachment.

Cannot invoke "String.equals(Object)" because "originalFileName" is null

Do you have any idea what this error is about?

entry.title: Re: Alternative labeling for function location & replacin...
entry.content:
In addition on the comments of Manfred,

Check if you have setup the inheritance of data in the Floc's or maintain the organizational data individual. But this could effect your mass change process. If the inheritance is on then you could change the top level and changes are passed to the lower Flocs until it is maintained individual. Be aware of any record locks in the process.

Also i believe it is not possible to change the label of a Floc in the same structure indicator in one go. So you will probably need to change to another structure indicator with the same mask or change to a dummy before making the final change.

Hope this helps

entry.title: Re: Missing packages for S/4 Migration in Maintenance Pla...
entry.content:
HI.

Unfortunately no. Theres a note from SAP that you have to wait with your patches in the system until the migration. but we have IS-H in our system (later in a sidecarsystem) and we cannot stop patching IS-H until the migration. 

We have asked the SAP month ago how to deal with that and they dont answer.

So - in your case, you have to stop patching about 6 months before the migration.
With IS-H im not allowed to wait with the patches.. SAP is ****

entry.title: Re: How to use Action in a Task automation
entry.content:
You are correct that Actions cannot be directly invoked within Task automation, but instead, they need to be invoked within a Process.

Here’s a step-by-step guide to solve your problem:

  1. Create Input Variables for the Process:

    • First, you need to define the input variables that will pass data to the Process.
    • To do this, open your Process, go to the 'Variables' tab (typically found in the right panel), and create the necessary input variables that correspond to the parameters your Action requires.

  2. Invoke the Process in the Task Automation:

    • In your Task automation, you should be able to add a Process activity.
    • Once you’ve added the Process, click on it and check for options to bind or map the input variables of the Process to the values from your Task automation.

     Regards,
     Penugonda Koushik

entry.title: Re: ALMathon 2024 for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: Looking forward to seeing these demo'd at the ALM Summit in Manneheim 💪

entry.title: Re: I'm facing issue while registering for External Authe...

entry.title: SAP Business Warehouse connect problem
entry.content:
I want to connect power bi SAP Business Warehouse but ı have this issue. SAP .net connector problem.

EN: The cause of this error may be vendor-specific client software that is required but not available on the computer. To download the client software for this vendor, visit the following site and select at least the 64-bit (x64) version of ‘SAP .NET Connector 3.0.0.42 / SAP .NET Connector 3.1.0.42’

ı have this version of .net connector, my boss have same version but he dont have same issue.



I can't find the .net connector version specified in the error. How can ı fix this ?

entry.title: Re: How to install DTW on client/workstation PC's with SA...

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA application shows Blank Page
entry.content:
Hi Reshma,

Please verify your lobby link to ensure it's correct. A typical SAP BPA lobby link should end with /lobby, like this:

https://<your_bpa_tenant_id>.us.<region>.cloud.sap/lobby

In your case, https://40a502d8trail.us.10build.cloud.sap/lobby 

Make sure you're using the correct link. If it’s still showing a blank page, it might be related to the roles you've assigned. Kindly share the roles you've defined so I can help you ensure that the necessary permissions are in place.

Additionally, try clearing your browser cache or accessing the BPA application using a different browser to rule out any caching issues.

Let me know if this helps!

Regards,
Penugonda Koushik

entry.title: Re: Adobe Document Services on SLES 15 SP6 non latin char...
entry.content:
Hi Marcin,

Check this SAP Note 2472828

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: How many user use Single SAP B1 License at same time...
entry.content:
Hi Nikunj,

It depends on the version of SAP Business One. With versions older than 9, two users could use the client at the same time with the same ID. With newer versions SAP has made it more difficult. Now two clients can only be open on the same computer.

SAP does not offer a license that allows multiple users to use the same ID on different computers.

Your have two options:

  1. Buy licenses for all users, who use the client.
  2. Build your own client that uses the service layer to interact with SAP Business One

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting 431 Status Code When Creating a SAMLA...
entry.content:
Hi,

We faced the same issue and applied the suggestion of Gregor by making the saml assertion token smaller. We achieved this  by limiting the user groups or ad groups in the saml ticket depending on the connected system. We provide a unique identifier to the company idp that will be used to only return a subset of ad groups instead of all.

entry.title: DRC - no data selected by "Switzerland VAT return (CH_VAT_DCL)"
entry.content:
Dear all,

We are trying to implement DRC (document reporting and compliance) in your S4HANA ON PREMISE (2022) system. From my point of view, we have completed the customizing (SPRO path Switzerland->Statutory Reportring->VAT Return->Setting up your statutory reporting), i.e. we have created a new reporting entity, assigned the report categories & org values etc. Additionally, we have completed the tax code mapping settings.

When starting the DRC dashboard, the report (Generate VAT return) is visible and can be started. The result/Document status for the selected period is "Generated Successfully". Anyhow, the XML has no content. I know that there is data in the selected period, because the standard VAT report (RFUMSV00) returns the expected document.

If I am not mistaken, one of the underlying CDS views is I_STRPTAXITEM which contains data as well.

We would like to know how we can debug the selection criteria to figure out the root cause for the missing data. Ideally, DRC would show more data other than just the company code and the date range... For whatever reason it must have excluded the relevant accounting documents. Ideally there is a report which can be used for analyses.

Any proposals?
Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Re: Message Definition like Service interface in Sproxy i...
entry.content:
for setting up existing ABAP Proxy scenarios on Cloud Integration, see https://help.sap.com/docs/migration-guide-po/migration-guide-for-sap-process-orchestration/setting-up-xi-proxy-runtime

For new proxies, there is a roadmap item: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=000D3A47875C1EDB98A8A910864AC24B&q=proxy&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=4B1ACEFEF2C91EDEB9D11F091B0E49FE

Alex

entry.title: Re: how to configure custom v2 service in the mobile connectivity in mdk application
entry.content: Please share the URL payload along with the error screenshot

entry.title: Re: Issue displaying data in item list in Build Apps
entry.content: Is your target deployment the SAP BTP? Then the authentication part should be handled by the Identity Provider configured for the BTP Subaccount.

entry.title: Re: Send email when Inbound IDOC fails
entry.content:
If your inbound process code has a workflow setup - e.g. 

You are able to trigger SAP workflows whenever an error occurred. Per default, it picks up the receiver of the workflow from FM EDI_ROLE_FOR_PROCESSING. So you can use the user you configure in WE20

now you have a workflow in the inbox in SAP. To trigger e-mails you need to schedule report RSWUWFML2 and it will send either for each individual workitem or for the list of workitems a mail with a direct link to SAP.

Hope that helps a bit.

BR Helmut

entry.title: Re: How to Set Up the Email Notification for Flexible Workflow in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Rosaria,

Thanks for the great blog. Is this also working for S4HANA public cloud? I was able to set up the email notification and template for the credit note approval SWF_CRT_NOTIFIY_RECIPIENTS. I used the name YY1_WS02000029_CRT_ALL

However with SWF_WORKFLOW_COMPLETE_NOTIFY I have issues. When I want to use below namespace it is not working.

  • <YY1>_<Scenario_id>_COMPLETE_POSITIVE
  • <YY1>_<Scenario_id>_COMPLETE_NEGATIVE

It looks like the field is to short. When I use YY1_020000029_Complete_Positive it is not working. and YY1_WS020000029_Complete_Positive  does not fit. Any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: How to replace BW/HANA Composite Provider with new So...
entry.content:
Hi Kiran

The way to do is to add the new source to the Union node. 

Then you can choose your new node in the source pane and chose Create Assignment Like (your old source)

Finish be removing your old source.

With kind regards

Kristian

entry.title: Re: how to configure custom v2 service in the mobile conn...
entry.content:
Hi @tirumala 

What you've entered is a relative service path name. You need to provide a full URL that begins with http:// E.g. http://host:port/sap/opu/odata/SAP/Zapp_dfrt_SRV

If you don't have the exact URL check with your system administrator.

Refer to this for more information on how to create a destination: https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/backend-connectivity/admin/config.html#creating-a-destination

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Automating Payment Proposal Process (F110) Through Batch Jobs For Program 'RFF110S'
entry.content: @nimish_agarwal In Step 2, for creating dynamic variant what values shall we enter for RUN ID. If we give company code and if we run in background system is giving error message "Run Identification Already Exists"

entry.title: Re: SAP BODS Connectivity with BW on Cloud
entry.content: I assum your BW will still the same BW as before. But its server machine is moved to a cloud platform. BW server host name/IP may be changed. If this is the case, you can just update new BW server host name in existing BW datastore. Make sure new BW server can communicate with DS Server.

entry.title: Calculate share in SAC on total amount for article
entry.content:
Hello!

I have a question I wonder if anyone can help me with.

In SAC I have a dashboard that calculates share of sales. Both for a dimension customer pricing group in one dashboard and article in the other.

When I click on one customer pricing group it will filter this in the article dashboard.

The share in the article dashboard is now calculated based on the sales for the article on the customer pricing group combined with the total sales for the customer pricing group.

What I want to accomplish instead is to have a share based on the sales for the article on the customer pricing group combined with total sales for the article despite of the filter of customer pricing group.

Does anyone know how to do this? We are using SAC on a live data connection based on a bex query.

Thanks!

/Maria

DB: MSSQL 13.00.2218.00

EHP7 FOR SAP ERP 6.0

entry.title: Re: Subsequent FI document not generating
entry.content:
Hello @Sreenath2 

As per note 382797 - MIRO: Analysis and repair for missing follow-on documents

If you set the indicator 'P_UPDATE' to 'X', the report changes the status of the selected incorrect documents to 'on hold'. Afterwards, you can use the standard transactions to either delete or continue processing the changed documents.

The ZREPMIR7 report doesn't post missing FI documents. It only sets "on hold" status on purchasing invoices, then you need to process them with standard transactions to post subsequent FI documents.

If you still can't post FI documents, see the note 2009994 - Error RW011: No subsequent document found in accounting - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA, especially point 4.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: need to understand that once you load the data to S4 GL balances via DMC, is there way to reload it
entry.content: need to understand that once you load the data to S4 GL balances via DMC, is there way to reload it, what would be the repercussions and what checks do we need to perform. Appreciating with thanks in advance.

entry.title: Down payments in Form Template SDBIL_CI_STANDARD_DE
entry.content:
Dear Team,

In the standard SAP content form in adobe livecycle designer, there is a section for downpayments in Form Template SDBIL_CI_STANDARD_DE.

How is this calculated? Is there any BADI to update this table when calling the invoice form output?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Down payments in Form Template SDBIL_CI_STANDARD_DE
entry.content:
Dear customer,

The BADI SD_BIL_PRINT_STANDARD_HEAD is able to modify custom header and item fields in the standard output form for billing documents.

Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for Sales Billing | SAP Help Portal

However it can only modify custom fields.

There is no dedicated BADI to influence the value for down payment related fields for billing documents.

As for how the fields are calculated, as it's out of the scope of standard support, I am providing you with following documentations for reference:

  1. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/466fb6535fe6b74ce10000000a174cb4.html
  2. https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2408?region=DE
  3. 3280969 - Wrong 'Open Total Amount' is printed on the down payment final invoice - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Error "Create an application copy. There is no copy m...
entry.content:
Dear Jörg,

Please check the newly available documentation below and compare your prerequisites for setting rules for Official Document Numbers.

There are now mandatory prerequisite steps for using the ODN Determination BRF application as a reference for all supported business processes. With this a SAP reference application must be copied into the customer specific application.

Basically without having system access further answering  this question would be heavily possible – so in case of an unresolvable error I would suggest to open an corresponding incident.

Best regards

Stefan

=============

Latest version:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/60a09f68f2444ceca31dcac2e7017945/6edf3adf10be469eaf14a53ad2a39bde.html?locale=en-US

Version of 2408 release:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/60a09f68f2444ceca31dcac2e7017945/6edf3adf10be469eaf14a53ad2a39bde.html?locale=de-DE&version=2408.500

entry.title: Re: EC Time and Attendance - new features Q4 2018
entry.content:
@Mona90 

Hello Simona,

but you agree that it is needed to create a break into a full-day absence record, right?

For half day absences (or other partial day absences) we plan to provide enhancements in the short/mid-term. You will be able to configure than more sophisticated the dynamic break process.

Best regards

Volker

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting 431 Status Code When Creating a SAMLA...
entry.content: In a CAP application you don't connect to HANA via a destination. You use a service binding.

entry.title: Re: A few updates from SAP Business One Support

SAP Business One version for SAP HANA - Database Administration Best Practices (English)

is not working.

entry.title: Re: Customer Consignment Process
entry.content: As the customer consignment stock is not on storage location level but on plant level, no storage location is required for the issue process.

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting 431 Status Code When Creating a SAMLA...
entry.content: I have created a backend system using CAPM and now I want to connect this system to Frontend system using destination and for this I want to use SAMLAssertion mechanism for security. While doing so I am encountering 431 error while checking connection in destination. What configuration do I have to implement in BAS to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: Over time calculation for Different WS hours in a sam...
entry.content:
Hello Somashree,

you can use the shift classification in the workschedule to differentiate both schedules. Afterwards you can use the "route" time valuation to distinguish both overtime calculation processes afterwards. The "route" time valuation type is like a kind of else / if-clause.

However, this would also be a good use case for our business rules in time valuation. We plan to release them as GA in H2. However, the fields that can be accessed are currently restricted, work schedule shift classification or workschedule is not included. But we plan in the future to enhance the accessible fields for business rules in time valuation and then you could use this way. However, right now there is no other option than to use the above mentioned way which leads unfortunately to a lot of time valuations to cover this.

Best regards

Volker

entry.title: How to obtain Valuation Type from Stock in Transit?
entry.content:
Dear all, 

We are facing an issue because we are not able to obtain the correct valuation type for stock that is in transit. 

Could anyone add some thoughts about how to navigate between tables in order to know which is the valuation type of any material that is in transit?

Thanks a lot

entry.title: SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.4 - What's new?
entry.content:
SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.4 is now available on the App Store. Let's have a look at the new features. 

With SAP Warehouse Operator 2.4, we are introducing these new key capabilities: 

  • Ad-Hoc warehouse task creation for Handling Units
  • New Targeted Scan to better select a specific barcode among many barcodes
  • Support for low stock checks when confirming warehouse tasks
  • Basic validation profiles to configure required validation fields based on warehouse number and process type
  • Picking of partial quantities from handling units

Ad-Hoc warehouse task creation for Handling Units

The creation of Ad-Hoc warehouse tasks for Handling Units is now supported. If you want to move a Handling Unit inside a warehouse, but no warehouse task has been created yet, you may now use the app to create the warehouse task directly on your iPhone. Simply by scanning the Handling Unit and defining the warehouse process type for the task.

You can then walk or drive to the destination bin in the warehouse and scan it with the mobile app to finalize the creation of the task. Of course, you may also directly drop-off the Handling Unit and confirm the task.

 

Targeted Scan

There are occasions in a warehouse, when you want to select a specific barcode among many barcodes. For example, when changing the destination bin or the resource. To better guide the user for these cases, we are introducing a Targeted Scan. A finder on the screen supports the user to better aim at specific barcodes. By tapping on a highlighted barcode on the screen, it is then scanned and selected.

 

Low Stock Checks

The low stock check is a physical inventory procedure that you perform when confirming warehouse taks. Here you assume that a storage bin should only contain a small quantity of a product after a stock removal in accordance with the storage bin data in EWM. You define this quantity in the form of a limit value that is based on the operative unit of measure for the product per storage type. 

The system automatically activates the low stock check as soon as you create a warehouse task for a storage bin that should lie below the defined limit value after the stock removal. After conforming the warehouse task in SAP Warehouse Operator, the mobile app asks you to enter the remaining stock quantity in the storage bin.

 

Validation Profiles

Sometimes, it’s not needed to scan and validate all data when working on a warehouse task. Maybe the product IDs have not been printed out as barcodes in your warehouse. With the help of simple configuration profiles, it’s now possible to define which data doesn’t have to be scanned and validated using the mobile app.   

An administrator may configure the validation file and store it in Mobile Services. The file then gets fetched during the onboarding of the app and the rules are applied to the handling of the warehouse tasks. The validation profiles allow you to skip validation for product IDs and batches specific to a process type and warehouse.  

 

Additional Features

We have also enabled picking from Handling Units. However, only the picking of partial quantities is supported. Full palette picking is not supported.  

SAP Warehouse Operator now also supports Branding and Theming. So, the app can be made available to employees of your company following a consistent user experience.

Do you want to give us feedback inside the mobile app? Please tap on the new Feedback button in the user profile, which will lead you to survey to provide feedback.

 

Try the app and engage with us

Please download the new version of the app and try it out yourself.

 

 

Please leave a comment below if you have questions or feedback and we’ll get back to you.

You may also contact us at SAP_Warehouse_Operator@sap.com if you have questions on the configuration to use the app, the setup process or have specific requirements.

We also encourage you to visit our SAP Mobile Applications community page and click “follow”. We’ll be publishing more informative blog posts. Please post questions in the SAP Extended Warehouse Management area. Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: Re: The benefits of integrating SAP Transportation Management with a Geographic Information System (
entry.content:
Hi Ashutosh_S, Nary_Razafintsa,

@Ashutosh_S : For sur the map cannot be displayed if you didn't register any map service in your system with an API key. You need to set up such steps in the transaction:

SPRO ==> Transportation Management ==> Basic Functions ==> Geographical Map ==> Visual Business ==> Maintain Application Definitions

You need to also ensure that the map is activated on the following customizing point: 

SPRO ==> Transportation Management ==> Basic Functions ==> Geographical Map ==> Define Settings for Geographical Map

Additional services in SICF need also to be activated, you can identify the missing services by activating the devtools on your internet browser and launching the map. 

@Nary_Razafintsa : Transportation lanes have their own value added into SAP TM such as running the automated planning process through the optimizer for example. So with just this example, we cannot say that GIS data can replace TL. The distances & duration of the TL are based on the means of transport. If you set up correctly the GIS integration, the Distance & Duration feature is triggered during TL creation.

If you plan without TL, the distance and duration can be requested on the freight order stages. 

For each request preparation (based on TL or FO stages) the system checks the buffer table of SAP TM before triggering the API, so it will minimize as much as possible the number of requests.

Hopefully it answers to your questions.

Best regards,

Adrien MANIER

entry.title: Re: Accidently deleted B1SiteUser
entry.content:
Dear yuyukyi 

To investigate this issue further, please refer to below mentioned steps:

1) Please take backup of SLD and B1AS database.

2) After this, take a downtime and perform clean uninstall of server tools using attached SAP note 2538555

Note: Do not forget to delete B1AS database before reinstalling server tools again.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh

entry.title: Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
The release of SAP Build's new extensibility wizard forSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release marks a significant step in streamlining the creation of on-stack extensions (within SAP S/4HANA Cloud) and side-by-side extensions (in SAP BTP) to help keep the core clean. The wizard, which can be accessed directly from any business application that is enabled for key user adaptation, allows key users and even developers to easily create custom process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) that can seamlessly integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. In this blog, we will explore the use cases for process extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how the new wizard is a game-changer for customers looking to enhance their business processes

Side-by-side extensions

Side-by-side extensions run outside of SAP S/4HANA Cloud (or other business application) but interact with the business applications using standard extension points like APIs, events, etc. Side-by-side extensions can technically run on any application platform, nevertheless SAP provides and recommends its dedicated platform for extension: SAP BTP.

What are process extensions

Many applications in SAP S/4HANA use SAP Business Workflow enabling preconfigured workflow scenarios to be reused in various situations. The scenarios can either be implemented without any changes or configured for your business processes by making minor adjustments. On top of that, Flexible workflows allow you to define condition-based workflows for the pre-configured approval processes. For example, the flexible workflow for purchase requisitions allows you to define one-step or multi-step approval processes for purchase requisitions according to your requirements. These are pre-delivered as part of the SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. However, your use case may go beyond a typical pre-delivered approval workflow which needs a side-by-side process extension. Here are some use case patterns which warrants a process extension in SAP BTP:

  • Processes that span across systems.
  • Processes that reach the users who are outside SAP S/4HANA.
  • Use certain capabilities from SAP BTP, like SAP AI Services.
  • Any other requirements that are not covered in the standard processes and workflows.

Example use case

Consider a specific use case where you want to make sure that every invoice created in the system has a reference to a purchase order without user intervention. There are multiple ways this can be achieved including a process extension. A side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (in SAP BTP) can check if a purchase order is assigned for every new supplier invoice created in the system. If not, then the process shall identify the right purchase order using SAP AI Services in SAP BTP like Document Information Extraction, Data Attribute Recommendation, Invoice Object Recommendation, SAP AI Core, etc. When the machine learning model could not find the right match, then it could inform the procurement department member for further action. This process extension would run alongside the standard procurement process, adding the necessary functionality without altering the original process.

Why SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation in SAP BTP with its rich development tools, integration capability and pre-built content is the recommended process extension platform for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. SAP Build Process Automation has 500+ pre-built content packages for extending SAP and non-SAP applications. Most of those content is free-to-use and can be accessed from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition 2408 release, the extensibility wizard streamlines the creation of process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation. The process created in SAP Build Process Automation is deployed & executable in SAP BTP and can be triggered automatically when a business event is observed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Creating a process extension

Let us see how easy it is to create a process extension with the new wizard.

Pre-requisite:

  • Setup SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh

A business event-based triggering of process is a capability of SAP Build Process Automation. It requires SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh to observe the events in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to initiate a process in SAP Build Process Automation. Refer to this blog for the detailed setup instruction.

  • You have the right business role and can access the extensibility wizard as described in previous blog of this series.

Procedure

  • From any application which is enabled for key user adaptation, open the 'Me Area' to choose the new 'Create Extension' option to launch the extensibility wizard.

Choose Process Extension. (If you do not have entitlement to SAP Build Process Automation in the connected SAP BTP tenant, then this option will be disabled with a 'Not subscribed' ribbon in the tile).

Based on the application from where the extension was triggered, the wizard automatically identifies the available extension points (in 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition, only business events are supported. More extension points is planned as future increments).

Choose Business Events, and proceed to next step.

The wizard also recognises the event objects that are relevant for the application and automatically applies a filter on the business object. Of course, you can override these filters and search any event that is needed for your use case. Select the appropriate event object and proceed to the next step.

All events of the selected event object are shown. Select the appropriate event and proceed to the next step.

Give a name and description for your extension project that will be created in SAP Build (SAP BTP). 

Confirm the changes in the Summary step or go back to edit any steps. This creates an extension project for based in the inputs given in the previous steps.

Once the project is created successfully, you can directly navigate to the project in SAP Build. 

When navigated into SAP Build (in SAP BTP), you can see a process automation project with a process created for you. 

When you open the process, you can see that the event selected in the wizard is automatically added. All the context from the selected business event is now available at your disposal. 

You can proceed to use the low-code process builder to create an extension process as needed. You can call any APIs like the SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP Cloud Integration models or any SAP BTP services, you can add a user task that creates a task in My Inbox or SAP Task Center, you can call an RPA bot automation, you can send notification emails, etc. Once the process is created, release a version and deploy the content into SAP BTP. 

When this process automation project is deployed in SAP BTP, it listens to the specific event in the connected SAP S/4HANA Cloud. When such an event is observed, it starts an instance of the process with the context that is available in the business event.

For more information on the lifecycle, refer to the help documentation of SAP Build Process Automation.

Key take aways

  • A brand-new wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is available with 2408 release to create on-stack and side-by-side extensions.
  • The wizard can be called from any application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition that is enabled for key user adaptation.
  • Discover the available process extension points based on the application that was used to start the wizard, through the concept of Business Context.
  • Create a side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Look-ahead plans

  • Management of side-by-side extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • More extension points for process extensions apart from the business event.
  • Enable the wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

entry.title: Re: Material Analysis Report
entry.content:
I agree with DominikTylczyn : Material Analysis and other types of Inventory Controlling reports have evolved with S/4HANA. It would be better to focus on the new possibilities / limitations ...

Kind regards

Nic T.

entry.title: Re: Outbound SMS / WhatsApp Message from SAP Service Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Kapil,

In our company we are using C4C and also have some experience with Twilio and also with Sinch.

I guess C4C's OData API would do just fine in this case + some simple middleware on Linux would be sufficient.

Roman

entry.title: i am using custom v2 service mdk after that i am getting HTTP status from the back end is 508
entry.content: i am using custom v2 service for btp mdk after that i am getting HTTP status from the back end is 508 error. checked the logs in that.

entry.title: Re: call classe methods below in another class code
entry.content: Use a CLASS cl_control DEFINITION DEFERRED before using it in the first class.

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Datasphere with SAP Integrated Business Planning, using SAP CI-DS - Part 3
entry.content:
Hi Domnic,

very good blog series, thanks a lot for your efforts. I have two questions about this option (Integrating via CI-DS using ODBC)

  1. Am I right in the assumption that by using CI-DS technically the connection from CI-DS to Datasphere is made by the SAP Data Services Agent acting as an SAp HANA client?
  2. Hence all traffic will effectively routed like this?
    • (IBP triggers CI-DS e,g. per Job) --> CI-DS --> Agent --> Datasphere --> Agent --> CI-DS --> IBP
  3. Regarding licenses: Does Datasphere license allow a HANA Client to connect to Datasphere? I know that SAP made quite a fuss for the on-premise HANA DB by separating full use license and application license, e.g. when having a BW/4HANA deployment. In my book for an on-premise BW/4HANA deployment one would need a HANA full use license to connecto to the HDB with a HANA client. I would be happy if SAP would allow this on Datasphere, though 🙂

Many thanks and kind regards

Jens

entry.title: ArchiveLink Attachments with Harmonized Document Management for Request for Quotations
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
im trying to use ArchiveLink attachments (via HDM) within the Fiori app F2049.
Currently i'm only able to use DMS attachments. I hope someone can help me with this.

The configuration table /view (V_AO_TYPE_DEF) includes the Objecttype 'RequestForQuotation', the object type 'BUS2010' and the TCLO-object 'EKKO_RFQ'.

Within the HDM_CUST i cannot activate ArchiveLink attachments as i get an warning that ArchiveLink is not valid.

Best Regards,
Julian

BR
Nicolas

entry.title: Re: REV.EXE file usage in SAP work directory
entry.content:
Hi Selle,

I checked in my SAP system which is running Windows 2016 OS with MS Sql 2016, I can't find the file name rev.exe, so you can simply move the file from SAP folder to some other temp folder, and then if possible, do the full system restart so that it will cleared your query.

Regards

SS

entry.title: File download no longer working in CAP 8
entry.content:
Hi community,

we are in the progress of migrating our CAP7 application to CAP8. In our app we have a function to download an asset from the BTP object store defined like this:

    this.on(download, async (req: Request) => {
      const fileBuffer = await this.objectStoreService.read(
        `assets/${req.data.assetId}`,
      );

      req.http?.res.setHeader('Content-Type', asset.mediaType!);
      req.http?.res.setHeader(
        'Content-Disposition',
        `inline;filename=${asset.fileName}`,
      );
      req.http?.res.send(fileBuffer);
    });

In CAP7 this code worked fine, but now it's giving the following error:

[cds] - Error: Cannot set headers after they are sent to the client

Actually, even the following simple code will now give the error above.

this.on(download, async (req: Request) => {
  req.http?.res.send('OK');
}

Any recommendations on how to implement a file download with CAP8 now?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track PORTO 2024

Pedro

entry.title: SAP Integrated Business Planning for Inventory Fundamentals
entry.content:
Are you looking to optimize your inventory management and improve your supply chain performance? If so, we have just the e-learning course for you. Introducing SAP Integrated Business Planning for Inventory Fundamentals offered by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy.

This comprehensive e-learning course will provide you with the knowledge and skills you need to effectively manage your inventory targets, balance risk, and maximize customer service levels. Whether you are new to SAP IBP for inventory as part of SAP Digital Supply Chain or looking to expand your knowledge, this course is designed to meet your needs.

Throughout this course, you will learn about inventory target calculation options and factors, basic data requirements, the impact of forecast error on safety stock, and much more. You will also gain valuable insights into inventory optimization key fundamentals and learn how to set up workflows to execute inventory optimization in your system.

By enrolling in this course, you will gain an overview of right-sizing inventory, understand how to calculate safety stocks, and learn how to effectively utilize IBP-Inventory to improve your inventory management processes.

The course covers the following topics:

  • Inventory target calculations and factors
  • Basic data requirements
  • Impact of demand variability on inventory target
  • Multi-stage concept
  • Impact of supply variability on inventory target

With our easy-to-follow, self-paced e-learning format, you can access the course material at your convenience and learn at your own pace. Our experienced instructors will guide you through the content, providing you with real-world examples and practical insights that you can apply to your own business.

So, if you are ready to take your inventory management skills to the next level, enroll in our SAP Integrated Business Planning for Inventory Fundamentals e-learning course today. Join us and gain the knowledge and expertise you need to optimize your inventory management and drive success in your supply chain.

Please note:
Before you can access the link to the e-learning course, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

 Click here to access the e-learning:

SAP Integrated Business Planning for Inventory Fundamentals

entry.title: Re: Asset Write-up (ABZU) for Prior Fiscal year in Prior...
entry.content:
If you can't find a suitable transaction code

  • (which ones did you try and what error(s) did you get?)

You could allow negative depreciation (select all allowed values) in OABN and create a transaction code by copying unplanned depreciation (640 of the asset is acquired in previous years) and use it?

entry.title: Looking for app developers who monitor apps they deployed
entry.content:
Hi,

We're working on a new dashboard for application monitoring for frontend, backend, and fullstack developers. 

I'm looking for developers who monitor the application they deployed for short interview to better understand what info you would expect to see and what actions you would expect to be available for you.

No need to prepare anything. Only meet for 30 min interview and impact on dashboard design.

Your feedback is highly appreciated.

Ravid (ravid.ellenbogen@sap.com)

entry.title: Re: Find API based interfaces in a SAP system in question
entry.content: This is not an answer to your question, right? In order to clarify your question, you should use a "Comment", not an "Answer"...

entry.title: Looking for app developers who monitor apps they deployed
entry.content:
Hi,

We're working on a new dashboard for application monitoring for frontend, backend, and fullstack developers. 

I'm looking for developers who monitor the application they deployed for short interview to better understand what info you would expect to see and what actions you would expect to be available for you.

No need to prepare anything. Only meet for 30 min interview and impact on dashboard design.

Your feedback is highly appreciated.

Ravid (ravid.ellenbogen@sap.com)

entry.title: Re: Story filter becomes open for user modification after...
entry.content:
@ivan_camac 

Hi Ivan,

Thanks for the comment. I understand your point of view, but then I would argue that if we count interactions through the API as user-edits, then it shouldn't be possible at all, as the filter is set to be non-editable. 

In our organisation, story users and story designers are two separate groups. We use the API if we want to perform some actions "behind the scenes" to ensure that the less proficient users have easy access to the data they need.

In other stories we do similar setup to what you mentioned - we block the access to the filters completely, and manage everything through the API. But in this case, the mixed access seemed to make more sense. Unfortunately it is not possible due to the unexpected API behaviour, so for now we will hide all the filters completely. 

Cheers, Łukasz

entry.title: Re: Asset Viewer Object Navigator Config
entry.content: Hi Tim, Thank you for your reply. I have seen these BF's are currently deactivated so i will activate and look again. Much appreciated with the help, Regards Tyrone

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP: How to add query parameters to S/4 backend call
entry.content:
I suppose these parameters are fixed for a connection. If so, you can add the following additional properties to the destination.

URL.queries.BackendUser=00010000000003
URL.queries.Scenario=D

The parameters  will get added to the URL as query parameters.
